reasoning: The tournament has reached the final, and the market makes Spain the clear favorite: they are priced around 8/13 (roughly -164) versus Argentina's 13/10, after eliminating France 2-0 in the semifinal. Spain carry the best pedigree and form of anyone left, as reigning European champions unbeaten in 37 straight games (tying the all-time national-team record) with the best defensive record in the competition, conceding just one goal in seven matches behind an elite spine of Cubarsi, Laporte, Rodri, Pedri and Yamal. The main threat is defending champion Argentina, who showed knockout resilience by erasing a 1-0 deficit to beat England late, with Messi's scoring surge keeping them dangerous in a one-off final. Only two teams remain live, so probabilities reflect the head-to-head; the risk to Spain is that short odds leave little margin against a battle-tested opponent, though their control and depth should decide it.
